name: Ortigueira — circular by Costa Norte and Playa de Morouzos
name_original: Ortigueira — circular por Costa Norte y Playa de Morouzos
region_intro: Jogging around Ortigueira provides diverse terrain across its northern Galician coastline, characterized by a mix of mountains, rivers, and the sea. The area features the wide Ortigueira and Ladrido estuaries, along with impressive cliffs and sandy beaches. Runners can explore coastal paths, riverbanks, and woodlands, offering varied landscapes for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Ortigueira The most popular jogging route is Ortigueira — circular by Costa Norte and Playa de Morouzos, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the coastline and Morouzos Beach. Another top favourite among local runners is Medieval defense tower – Loiba Cliff, Picón loop from Estaca de Bares SAC, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This route features coastal scenery and passes by the Loiba Cliff. Local runners also love the Loiba Cliff, Picón – Medieval defense tower loop from Loiba, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Ortigueira is defined by coastal paths, river estuaries, and impressive cliffs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Ortigueira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Ortigueira?
A: There are over 35 running routes around Ortigueira, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these trails more than 200 times, highlighting the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there running trails suitable for beginners in Ortigueira?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, Ortigueira offers paths suitable for beginners. These often feature less elevation gain and cover shorter distances, allowing new runners to enjoy the scenic coastal paths and riverbanks at a comfortable pace.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Ortigueira?
A: Running routes in Ortigueira feature a diverse mix of terrain. You can expect coastal paths with dramatic cliff views, trails along the wide Ortigueira and Ladrido estuaries, and paths winding through woodlands. Some routes also include sandy stretches along beautiful beaches like Morouzos Beach.

Q: Can I find routes with waterfalls in the Ortigueira area?
A: Absolutely! The inland landscape of Ortigueira features beautiful waterfalls. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is accessible via various loops, adding a refreshing element to your run. The Region Research also mentions the Pozo do Inferno Waterfall.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or landmarks to see while running?
A: Ortigueira is rich in scenic viewpoints and landmarks. Many running routes offer stunning vistas from impressive cliffs like the Loiba Cliffs and Vixía Herbeira. You might also encounter highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/425141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garita Herbeira (611m)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3479074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape Ortegal Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3175098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Estaca de Bares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails around Ortigueira suitable for families?
A: Many of Ortigueira's trails are suitable for families, especially those looking for easier, accessible paths. Routes along the estuaries or beaches like Morouzos Beach offer flatter, safer options for a family run or walk, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Ortigueira?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in Ortigueira, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in nature protection areas or when encountering wildlife, and to follow local regulations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Ortigueira?
A: The generally temperate climate of Ortigueira, with drier summers, makes spring and autumn particularly pleasant for outdoor activities like running. The milder temperatures and vibrant scenery during these seasons offer ideal conditions, though summer also provides opportunities for coastal runs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Ortigueira?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Ortigueira are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984063078/ortigueira-circular-by-costa-norte-and-playa-de-morouzos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ortigueira — circular by Costa Norte and Playa de Morouzos&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.3 km trail offering coastal views.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Ortigueira?
A: Runners frequently praise the diverse and stunning natural landscapes of Ortigueira. The combination of dramatic cliffs, beautiful beaches, and tranquil estuaries provides varied scenery. Many appreciate the options for different fitness levels and the opportunity to explore unique ecosystems and historical elements like portions of the Camino de Santiago.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Ortigueira offers more difficult routes with significant elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31889197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loiba Cliff, Picón – Medieval defense tower loop from Espasante&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 15.5 km route with over 300 meters of elevation gain.

Q: How can I access the running trails in Ortigueira?
A: Many running trails in Ortigueira are accessible by car, with parking often available near trailheads or popular starting points. For some routes, public transport might also be an option, especially for trails closer to urban centers or along main roads. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking and access information.
tips: Running path along the Ortigueira estuary, with spectacular views of the dunes of the estuary when the tide changes
You can run along the coast, along the sand or along the trails where the dunes meet the trees and vegetation. Spectacular ever-changing views of the estuary 365 days a year
